hero.intro: Independent product and interaction designer. I work at the seam of product, brand and motion — currently shipping small, honest things from Copenhagen.
hero.showcaseIntro: A scrollable slice of interface studies, systems work, and product screens.
hero.asideLede: Independent designer working between product, brand and interaction. Previously at studios you might know, now shipping small honest things from Copenhagen.

services.heading: What I do
services.intro: From product direction to interface detail, I work with teams that need a design partner who can think structurally and still care about the last 8 pixels.

featured.heading:
A handful of projects I'm still
quietly proud of.
featured.sub: Selected case studies across apps, websites, and systems work. Each one is here because the final thing feels sharper than what came before it.

projects.title: Reinventing the Everyday Voucher
projects.summary: A unified loyalty wallet that turns daily errands into a quiet reward — rebuilt around one tappable card and a light visual grammar.

blocks.label: Context
blocks.body:
A unified loyalty wallet that turns daily errands into a quiet reward — rebuilt around one tappable card and a light visual grammar.

The team came in with fragmented tools and a long list of feature requests. We responded by doing less: a shared language, fewer primitives, and a quieter front door that the product could finally grow around.

blocks.text: Two months of user interviews surfaced a single sentence that rewrote the product: "I want the card to just be ready when I'm at the register." Everything after that was in service of that sentence.

blocks.caption: Early synthesis wall — 240 interview moments clustered down to eleven.
blocks.alt: Research synthesis board with sticky notes

blocks.title: Design principle — quiet by default
blocks.body: Reward mechanics are exciting in meetings and exhausting in the hand. We used motion only when it confirmed a real thing happening.

blocks.text: They reframed the problem before they touched the pixels — and the product got measurably calmer as a result.
blocks.author: — Head of Product, Pointvoucher

items.label: Timeline
items.value: 12 weeks from research to release candidate.

items.label: Scope
items.value: Wallet flows, rewards states, token system.

cards.v: Diary studies with 14 users over three weeks.
A single set of spacing and type tokens.
Collapsing four onboarding screens into two.

blocks.label: Outcomes
blocks.body: Activation up 41%, support tickets down 28%, and a team that finally trusts its own component library. The product got simpler; the people, less tired.

blocks.heading: Three things the redesign made easier
items: Users could reach their active card with less visual noise and fewer taps.
Merchant-facing states finally behaved consistently across loyalty, rewards, and expired offers.
The product team got a reusable type and spacing system instead of another one-off launch layer.

items.v: Interview synthesis, competitor teardown, and a fresh jobs-to-be-done statement.
Rapid interaction studies in Figma and code to de-risk the wallet and checkout states.
System cleanup, QA support, and instrumentation planning for rollout.

projects.title: Shaping a Voice Worth Hearing
projects.summary: A quiet editorial site for a composer — typography doing the heavy lifting, sound sitting comfortably beside the image.

blocks.label: Context
blocks.body:
A quiet editorial site for a composer — typography doing the heavy lifting, sound sitting comfortably beside the image.

Voice-first design for a classical composer. We set the type first, then asked what sound would earn a place on the same page.

blocks.caption: A type-led homepage — sound is an afterthought until you hover.

blocks.text: He gave me an internet that finally felt like mine — patient, legible, and quiet.
blocks.author: — Rasmus Noah Hansen

cards.v: Three conversations, one studio visit, two tea breaks.
Setting a voice on the page before touching layout.
Hover audio peeks; no autoplay, ever.
Removing two nav items and one hero video.

blocks.label: Outcomes
blocks.body: Bounce rate down, session time doubled, and a composer who is finally proud to share his own URL.

projects.title: Love, Endorsed by your Inner Circle
projects.summary: A dating concept built on trust — friends introduce friends. Warm, tactile, and deliberately slow.

blocks.label: Context
blocks.body:
A dating concept built on trust — friends introduce friends. Warm, tactile, and deliberately slow.

We designed a dating app that made speed feel undignified. Everything in it is a trade: your attention for your friends' care.

blocks.title: Core idea — slowness as a feature
blocks.body: One introduction per day. No explore feed. Your friends do the filtering. The entire app sits behind a single human-scale decision.

blocks.text: For the first time it felt like someone who knew me was doing the swiping.
blocks.author: — Beta user, Copenhagen

blocks.label: Outcomes
blocks.body: D30 retention 3.4× the dating app average; a quieter inbox that users opened voluntarily.

projects.title: Connecting Minds to Accelerate Innovation
projects.summary: A platform for founders and scientists — threading collaboration, research and funding into one coherent surface.

blocks.label: Context
blocks.body:
A platform for founders and scientists — threading collaboration, research and funding into one coherent surface.

Three separate platforms were consolidated into one — without losing the specific rituals of each community.

blocks.text: They built us a grammar we could argue in — and the arguments finally converged.
blocks.author: — Platform Lead, BII

cards.v: One week mapping overlaps across three products.
A typography and spacing scale both audiences agreed on.
Contribution rules for a fifteen-person team.

blocks.label: Outcomes
blocks.body: Onboarding time halved, support tickets down by a third, and a shared language that survived the redesign.

projects.title: A Calmer Way to Move Your Money
projects.summary: A consumer banking app that replaces dashboards with one honest question: what are you trying to do today?

blocks.label: Context
blocks.body:
A consumer banking app that replaces dashboards with one honest question: what are you trying to do today?

Banking apps default to data walls. We tried a softer front door — one that asked, and then answered.

blocks.text: Our least-stressed users are now our most active ones.
blocks.author: — CPO, Northbound

blocks.label: Outcomes
blocks.body: NPS up 22, weekly active up 18%, support tone warmer. Not every bank needs more buttons.

projects.title: A Studio Identity, Woven in Paper
projects.summary: A small studio identity that refuses a mark — letters set in lead, type wandering between print and pixel.

blocks.label: Context
blocks.body:
A small studio identity that refuses a mark — letters set in lead, type wandering between print and pixel.

A two-person studio with fifteen notebooks and no logo. We built an identity from the margins of those books.

blocks.text: It felt like they found our identity, not invented it.
blocks.author: — Co-founder, Field & Form

blocks.label: Outcomes
blocks.body: Two studios merged. One voice. Zero logos.

items.v: Tokens, components, governance; built to outlast me.
Figma, code, motion. Resolving arguments the fast way.
Type, image, pacing — tone before features.
GSAP, Lottie, Rive. Movement with a reason to move.

aboutTeaser.heading: I believe good interfaces are the ones you don't notice — the ones that move out of the way so the work can speak.
aboutTeaser.sub: Fifteen years in, I'm still drawn to the small moments: the hover that confirms, the transition that reassures, the line of type that carries a whole brand on its back.

archive.heading:
Everything else — fifty-six
projects, in plain text.
archive.sub: Shorter notes on shipped work. Hover a row to peek.

published.heading:
Essays, talks and the odd
argument with myself.

items.t: On slowness and the design of restraint
What we keep when we ship

bio: Hi — I'm Atulya. I design products and identities that try to do less, better. My work sits at the intersection of interaction, type and tone. I care about legibility, pacing, and the small sound a button makes when it's been considered.

I grew up between cities — Mumbai, Berlin, Copenhagen — and most of what I know I learned from the people around me. Before going independent I spent eight years in product studios, shipping across finance, health, media and the quiet kind of consumer apps you keep on your home screen.
Outside of client work I write essays, teach the occasional workshop, and keep a shelf of handmade notebooks that never quite get used.

footer.lead: Currently taking on two new projects for autumn — interaction-led product work, thoughtful brand systems, and long-form case studies.
